text.title: Gcp Billing Cost Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-billing-cost methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Every billing account MUST have BigQuery billing export enabled (detailed-usage + pricing tables).
rule.rationale: Without BQ export, cost analysis is limited to the Cloud Console UI — no joinable, queryable fact table.

rule.statement: Every project MUST have budget alerts at 50%, 90%, and 100% of monthly target with Pub/Sub notification.
rule.rationale: Tiered alerts give time to react before overspend; Pub/Sub channel enables automation (Slack, paging).

rule.statement: All resources MUST carry labels `env`, `team`, `cost-center` at minimum; missing labels block cost attribution.
rule.rationale: Without labels the BQ export aggregates only by project — too coarse to allocate cost to teams.

rule.statement: Stable baseline compute (vCPU/RAM, Cloud SQL) MUST be evaluated for 1y/3y committed-use discounts quarterly.
rule.rationale: CUDs deliver 30–55% discount on baseline; skipping the review leaves money on the table.

rule.statement: Idle resources (zero-traffic Cloud Run, unattached IPs, oversized Cloud SQL, idle GCE) MUST be reviewed monthly via Recommender.
rule.rationale: Recommender surfaces idle/oversized resources automatically; ignoring it accumulates waste over time.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
